perturbation.original_text: If CONSULTANT disapproves, the reasons therefore shall be given to ADAMS GOLF in writing within three (3) business days or shall be deemed approved.
perturbation.changed_text: If CONSULTANT disapproves, the reasons therefore shall be given to ADAMS GOLF in writing within one (1) business day or shall be deemed approved.
perturbation.explanation: Shortening the response time to one business day could be considered an unreasonably short timeframe, potentially conflicting with general contract law principles regarding reasonable time for review and response, especially given that Kansas law governs the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_law: General Contract Law - Reasonable Time for Review and Response
perturbation.law_explanation: While not a specific statute in Kansas, reducing the time to one business day might be deemed unreasonable under general contract law principles, especially if the advertising material requires substantial review. This is due to the fact that a 'reasonable time' should be afforded and 1 day is too short for substantial review.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: Penn WebLogin Log in with your PennKey Username Password Log in Forgot username? Forgot password? Change my password Test my PennKey Privacy policy
perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the response time from three business days to one business day. The Perturbation Explanation and Law Explanation suggest this could be deemed unreasonable under general contract law principles, especially if the material requires substantial review. This creates a potential conflict because a 'reasonable time' should be afforded for review, and one day is argued to be insufficient. Although the snippets provided are unhelpful, the combination of the original/changed text and the Perturbation/Law explanations point towards a contradiction. The change creates a deadline that may violate state law.

perturbation.original_text: Every written notice or written report which may be served upon CONSULTANT, according to the terms of this Agreement, may be served by enclosing it in a postpaid envelope addressed to: Mr. Tom Watson C/O Assured Management Company 1901 W. 47th Place, Suite 200 Westwood, Kansas 66205 or at such other address as is given in writing to ADAMS GOLF by CONSULTANT.
perturbation.changed_text: Every written notice or written report which may be served upon CONSULTANT, according to the terms of this Agreement, may be served via telepathy to: Mr. Tom Watson C/O Assured Management Company 1901 W. 47th Place, Suite 200 Westwood, Kansas 66205 or at such other address as is given in writing to ADAMS GOLF by CONSULTANT.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the method of written notice to telepathy is not a valid method of communication according to the law, and creates an unenforceable contract.
perturbation.law_explanation: The UETA and common law require that notices be delivered via a reliable method, of which telepathy is not. A notice needs to be reasonably proven to be delivered.

perturbation.justification: The change from written notice to 'telepathy' directly contradicts the requirement for a reliable method of delivery for legal notices. The Law Explanation explicitly states that 'The UETA and common law require that notices be delivered via a reliable method, of which telepathy is not' and that 'A notice needs to be reasonably proven to be delivered.' Telepathy is not a reliable or provable method of delivery. While the provided scraped snippets do not directly address the method of service, the Law Explanation provides a sufficient legal basis to determine the change introduces a legal contradiction, as it is likely unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: The arbitration shall be conducted in accordance with the Arbitration Rules of the American Arbitration Association. Venue shall be Kansas.
perturbation.changed_text: The arbitration shall be conducted in accordance with the Arbitration Rules of the Klingon Arbitration Association. Venue shall be Qo'noS.
perturbation.explanation: Specifying a nonexistent arbitration body and a venue outside of any recognized legal jurisdiction makes the clause unenforceable. It directly contradicts the intention to create a binding arbitration agreement.
perturbation.law_explanation: The FAA requires that arbitration agreements be enforceable and within a recognized legal framework. Specifying a nonexistent arbitration body and an impossible venue renders the agreement unenforceable, as there's no legal mechanism to enforce rulings from the 'Klingon Arbitration Association' or to compel attendance at a venue on 'Qo'noS'.

(1/4/1995) Back to Original Document << Previous TITLE 9 / CHAPTER 1 / § 1 Next >> [Print] [Print selection] [OLRC Home] Help 9 USC 1 : "Maritime transactions" and "commerce" defined; exceptions to operation of title Text contains those laws in effect on June 14, 2025 From Title 9-ARBITRATION CHAPTER 1-GENERAL PROVISIONS Jump To: Source Credit Miscellaneous Short Title §1. "Maritime transactions" and "commerce" defined; exceptions to operation of title "Maritime transactions", as herein defined, means charter parties, bills of lading of water carriers, agreements relating to wharfage, supplies furnished vessels or repairs to vessels, collisions, or any other matters in foreign commerce which, if the subject of controversy, would be embraced within admiralty jurisdictio
perturbation.justification: The changed text specifies arbitration under the 'Klingon Arbitration Association' with venue on 'Qo'noS,' which are nonexistent legal entities and locations. This renders the arbitration agreement unenforceable because there is no recognized legal framework to enforce rulings or compel attendance. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 primarily defines 'maritime transactions' and 'commerce' under the FAA, the FAA's underlying requirement for an enforceable arbitration agreement is undermined by specifying an impossible arbitration body and venue. The original text, specifying the American Arbitration Association and Kansas as the venue, creates a legally sound and enforceable agreement, unlike the changed text.
